rationales.power: Khalid claims credit for the protocol work and Tariq 'just sat there,' losing status and control in front of Dr. Fatima.
He is forced to wait while Hassan 'holds the cards,' and the loss of control over a ward need is what makes him feel stuck.
rationales.benevolence: That frustration spills onto Faisal when he snaps over homework even though he knows 'it had nothing to do with him.'

rationales.achievement: He solves the staffing problem competently enough that the ward gets through the day and Nour calls it the 'calmest crisis' she had been through.
rationales.power: He takes charge of the shortage by pulling everyone together, laying out the situation, and coordinating coverage without losing command of the room.
He gives Amira full responsibility for the audit and admits there may be more he does not need to hold onto 'so tightly,' loosening personal control.
rationales.benevolence: He publicly tells Amira her report was 'solid work,' giving a colleague trust and recognition.

rationales.self_direction: He brings his own data-based discharge protocol and openly pushes back against the regional template instead of simply accepting it.
rationales.power: Even after making the case, the decision stays with higher-ups and he ends up implementing a protocol he does not believe in.
He corrects the coordinator in front of the group to reassert his title and status as the charge nurse and ward lead.
Being talked over mid-sentence and then going silent leaves him without influence in the meeting where he was supposed to make the case.
rationales.conformity: He says doctors interrupting nurses is 'just how it is' and avoids pushing back because he fears being seen as 'the angry nurse.'

rationales.achievement: He treats the leadership conference seriously enough to submit the request, arrange his own coverage, and keep tracking the deadline.
rationales.power: Even after doing that work, attendance still depends on his department head's sign-off, so he is left waiting on someone else's priority.

rationales.achievement: He spends two evenings building a documented staffing case with patient numbers, acuity trends, and incident reports.
rationales.power: He concludes the real decision turns on access and relationships he does not have, leaving him outside the influential conversation.

rationales.achievement: Even after being disregarded, he says building the case well is 'who I am,' showing continued commitment to competence and preparation.
